genai.title: Gen Art
genai.description: Create a compelling 1-3 minute video leveraging AI images to tell a local story in a meaningful way.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.description: Design innovative learning environments that shape the future of education.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: A nationwide AI challenge for student teams to design, build, and present practical solutions. Expect hands-on problem solving, expert feedback, and workshops that sharpen your approach.

hero.subtitle: National AI Competition 2026
hero.title: Tracks
hero.desc: Explore our five distinct competition tracks for 2026. Find the path that best matches your passion and skills. Specific competition topics will be announced to registered teams on March 19, 2026.

headers.exclusiveAnnouncement: The specific competition topic will be announced exclusively to registered teams on March 19, 2026.

cta.join: Join hundreds of students across Malaysia in the largest student AI competition.

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.overview: Apply generative AI and create apps that offer unique solutions to key problems focusing on one of 3 selected UN SDG areas. Specific SDG areas and detailed requirements will be announced on March 19, 2026.
innovation.whoIsThisFor: Students passionate about using AI to solve real-world problems aligned with UN Sustainable Development Goals.
innovation.deliverables: A functional app or solution, a project proposal, and a presentation pitch.
innovation.example2: Generative AI solution for quality education access

judging1.title: Problem & Solution Fit
judging1.desc: How well the solution addresses a real problem within the selected SDG area.

judging2.title: Innovation & AI Integration
judging2.desc: Creative and effective use of generative AI in the solution.

judging3.title: Impact Potential
judging3.desc: The potential of the solution to create meaningful change.

computing.title: Computing
computing.overview: Prepare a high-quality pipeline and train an AI model that can make reliable predictions from medical images. The solution must handle realistic settings where data variability and real-world noise are common. Dataset details and technical specifications will be announced on March 19, 2026.
computing.whoIsThisFor: Enthusiastic participants who want to get hands-on with real-world medical data and push the limits of AI technologies.
computing.deliverables: Inference/Testing Script, Model Weights/Saved Model, and a Short Technical Write-Up.
computing.example1: Inference script that outputs diagnostic predictions
computing.example2: Model weights with architecture details and preprocessing config

judging1.title: Performance
judging1.desc: Accuracy, F1 Score, Precision, Recall, and Confusion Matrix performance.

judging2.title: Model Design
judging2.desc: Architecture choice, handling class imbalance, and generalization methods.

judging3.title: Pipeline Quality
judging3.desc: Data preprocessing, validation strategy, and reproducibility.

engineering.title: Engineering
engineering.overview: Design AI-powered engineering solutions that enhance efficiency and sustainability in our communities. Use technology to solve real-world physical challenges. Specific challenge details will be announced on March 19, 2026.
engineering.whoIsThisFor: Problem-solvers interested in AI-powered intelligent systems integrating hardware and software for problems involving robotics, automation, processes, and infrastructure.
engineering.deliverables: Proposal report, system design workflow, and a proof-of-concept prototype (hardware or software simulation).

judging1.title: Problem Understanding
judging1.desc: Clarity of the defined problem and its relevance.

judging2.title: AI-Enabled Solution
judging2.desc: Quality of solution and effectiveness of AI integration.

judging3.title: Prototype & Impact
judging3.desc: Demonstration of core idea and potential impact.

genai.title: Gen Art
genai.overview: Create a compelling 1-3 minute video leveraging AI images to tell a local story in a meaningful way. Specific story themes and submission guidelines will be announced on March 19, 2026.
genai.whoIsThisFor: Creative storytellers and AI enthusiasts looking to master short-form video content and AI-assisted visualization.
genai.deliverables: A 1-3 minute TikTok-style video, a prompt log for AI visuals, and a creative story statement.
genai.example1: Cinematic Journey through Malaysia's Hidden Food Gems
genai.example2: Legendary Tales of Malaysian Folklore Reimagined with AI

judging1.title: Visual Creativity
judging1.desc: Quality and consistency of AI-generated stills.

judging2.title: Storytelling
judging2.desc: Compelling use of script and voice-over narrative.

judging3.title: Technical Execution
judging3.desc: Quality of video editing and viral engagement potential.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.overview: Reimagine spaces for learning that prioritise flexibility, technology, and human comfort. Detailed design briefs and requirements will be announced on March 19, 2026.
architecture.whoIsThisFor: Visionaries interested in architecture, spatial design, and sustainable environments using AI tools.
architecture.deliverables: One A1 design board (PDF) including visuals, spatial diagrams (plan/section), and a design story.
architecture.example1: Inclusive Design for Diverse Learning Styles

judging1.title: Design Process
judging1.desc: AI prompts, sketches, and clarity of design thinking.

judging3.title: Storytelling
judging3.desc: Clarity of purpose and connection to the theme.

q2.question: Are students from private and international schools eligible?
q2.answer: Yes, we welcome students from all backgrounds.

q3.question: Is the AI Masterclass complimentary?
q3.answer: Yes, the AI masterclass is complimentary.

q4.question: How many tracks are there?
q4.answer: Five tracks: Innovation, Engineering, Gen Art, Computing, and Architecture.

q5.question: When do I know the competition topic?
q5.answer: Competition topics and materials will be shared with all registered teams on March 19, 2026.

hero.subtitle: National AI Competition 2026
hero.title: Frequently Asked
hero.desc: Everything you need to know about the National AI Competition 2026.

items.question: What is the National AI Competition?
items.answer: The National AI Competition is Malaysia's premier artificial intelligence competition for students from the upper-secondary to the pre-university (or equivalent) stage. Co-hosted by Rakan Tutor and Sunway University, this initiative aims to inspire and support AI application among Malaysian youth. The challenge provides a platform for students to showcase their AI solutions, promoting technological advancement and preparing them for future academic and professional pursuits.

items.question: What is the objective of the National AI Competition?
items.answer: To promote AI knowledge, encourage innovation, and inspire the next generation of tech leaders.

items.question: How does the competition work?
items.answer:
The National AI Competition consists of two rounds: an Preliminary round [online proposal submission] and Grand Finals at Sunway University [On-ground].

Here's how it works:
1. Registration: Register your team by 16 March 2026.
2. Competition Materials & AI Masterclass: Receive the competition topics and participate in the complimentary AI masterclass between 31 March 2026 – 2 April 2026.
3. Proposal Submission: Work on your project as a team and submit your proposal by 4 May 2026.
4. Finalists will be announced on 15 May 2026, 4pm and will compete at the Grand Finals at Sunway University on 13 June 2026. Refer to the timeline for detailed dates and steps.

items.question: How many rounds will there be?
items.answer: There will be a total of 2 rounds. The first round will be Proposal submission. Shortlisted participants will be invited to the Grand Finals at Sunway University.

items.question: How many tracks are there?
items.answer:
There are five tracks:
- Innovation: Apply generative AI and create apps that offer unique solutions to key problems focusing on one of 3 selected UN SDG areas.
- Engineering: Design AI-powered solutions to improve community efficiency and sustainability.
- Gen Art: Create a compelling 1-3 minute video leveraging AI images to tell a local story in a meaningful way.
- Computing: Develop an AI model for medical diagnostics.
- Architecture: Design innovative learning environments that shape the future of education.
Refer to the Track page to learn more

items.question: Where and when will the final be held?
items.answer: The grand finals will be held on June 13, 2026 at Sunway University, Sunway City.

items.question: Can I join remotely for the final?
items.answer: We highly encourage all finalists to attend the final round in person.

items.question: How many teams will be selected for the final?
items.answer: There will be 6 teams for each category, 12 teams for each track.

items.question: How do we shortlist teams for the final?
items.answer: Finalists will be selected based on the judging rubrics specific to their chosen track. Participants may refer to the competition booklet on the rubrics for the competition. The announcement of finalists will be on May 15, 2026.

items.question: Who can participate?
items.answer: Participants must be an active students from [Form 4 – Form 5, Year 10 – Year 11, Senior Middle 1 – Senior Middle 2] are eligible to join Category A; Participants from [STPM, UEC, Pre-University programmes and Diploma] are eligible to join Category B. Note: American Degree Programme students are not eligible as they are considered university students.

items.question: How do I join?
items.answer:
1. Check your eligibility.
2. Fill up the registration form.
Registration closes on March 16, 2026 23:59 MYT.

items.question: Do I have to pay to register to join the competition?
items.answer: No, there are no fees.

items.question: I don't know about AI. Can I join?
items.answer: Yes, you can join even if you don't know about AI. We provide complimentary Masterclasses and compile resources to help you prepare for the competition.

items.question: Is there a limit to the number of participants from each school?
items.answer: No, there is no limit to the number of participants from each school. A school can send multiple teams of 4, participating in different categories and tracks, without any restrictions. One student is able to join for only ONE (1) track, ONE (1) one category.

items.question: Can I have teams less than 4 people?
items.answer: No, all teams must consist of exactly 4 members.

items.question: Can I form a team with friends from other schools?
items.answer: Yes, you can form a team with friends from other schools. If any of the member falls under Category B: STPM, Pre-U, UEC, Diploma, the team will be competing under Category B. Note: American Degree Programme students are not eligible.

items.question: Can a team have students from different category?
items.answer: Yes, a team can have students from different school levels. However, If any of the member falls under Category B: STPM, Pre-U, UEC, Diploma, the team will be competing under Category B. Note: American Degree Programme students are not eligible.

items.question: Are students from private and international schools allowed?
items.answer: Yes, students from international and private schools are welcome to join!

items.question: Can non-Malaysians participate?
items.answer: Yes, non-Malaysians are welcome to participate, even if they do not reside in Malaysia.

items.question: Can I participate in more than one track?
items.answer: No, participants can only join one track.

items.question: I think I registered, but I'm not sure.
items.answer: If you think you registered but are not sure, check for a confirmation email. All registered teams will receive a confirmation email together with competition materials, themes, and masterclass access details on March 19, 2026 upon successful registration. If these updates have not been received by then, please email us at mingjackt@sunway.edu.my (Jack) / clement@sunway.edu.my (Clemen) for assistance.

items.question: Is a teacher advisor required?
items.answer: Yes. A teacher advisor is required.

items.question: Do I need coding experience for the competition?
items.answer:
It depends on the track you choose:
- Computing: Coding or technical experience is highly encouraged.
- Innovation, Engineering, Gen Art, and Architecture Tracks: Coding experience is not required. We focus on design, creativity, and problem-solving.

items.question: How do I prepare for the competition?
items.answer:
To prepare for the competition:
1. Attend the AI Masterclass provided to gain insights and skills.
2. Access additional complimentary resources shared by the organisers.

items.question: Is the AI Masterclass complimentary?
items.answer: Yes, the AI Masterclass is complimentary. Each track will have a complimentary AI Masterclass.

items.question: How many AI Masterclasses are there?
items.answer: There is only one AI masterclass for each track.

items.question: Can I join the AI Masterclass for other tracks?
items.answer: You are only allowed to join the Masterclass that is related to the Track that you registered.

items.question: When is the AI Masterclass?
items.answer: Details will be announced soon, it will be one of the days between 31 March 2026 – 2 April 2026. Reminders will be shared in the WhatsApp group.

items.question: Will the AI Masterclass be recorded?
items.answer: Yes, but live attendance is encouraged for Q&A.

items.question: Will the AI Masterclass be physical or virtual?
items.answer: The AI Masterclass will be conducted virtually on Zoom.

items.question: If I can't make it to the AI Masterclass, does it disqualify me?
items.answer: No, it won't disqualify you. We highly encourage attending the AI Masterclass as it will help you prepare and allow you to ask questions during the Q&A. However, we understand if you can't make it. You can watch the recordings later, and even if you choose not to, it will not affect your participation in the competition.

items.question: Do all participants get a certificate of participation?
items.answer: Yes, all participants will be given a certificate of participation upon successful submission of the Preliminary round before the deadline.

items.question: What do winners get?
items.answer: Winners will receive Medals, Sunway Bursaries (Terms and Conditions apply) and more.

items.question: What is the method of communication?
items.answer: We will use a WhatsApp community group for team leaders and teacher advisors to share important updates. Additionally, we will send emails directly to participants. Please ensure you regularly check your email inbox (and spam / junk folder) to avoid missing any communications from us.

items.question: Where can I find the Terms and Conditions?
items.answer: You can find the full Terms and Conditions at the footer of our website. Click on 'Terms' at the bottom of any page to view all competition rules, eligibility requirements, and bursary conditions. You can also visit rakantutor.org/naic/terms directly.

items.question: What if I have further inquiries?
items.answer: If you have any further questions, please email mingjackt@sunway.edu.my (Jack) / clement@sunway.edu.my (Clemen) or WhatsApp Jack at (019-200 4268).

eligibility.title: Eligibility & Conditions of Entry
catAItems: Form 4 and Form 5 (National and Private Schools)
Senior Middle 1 and Senior Middle 2 (Chinese Independent Schools)
eligibility.catBNote: Note: American Degree Programme students are not eligible as they are considered university students.
eligibility.dateCondition: Only those whose graduation date falls after 13 June 2026 are eligible to participate in the Competition.
eligibility.statusCondition: This condition is set to ensure that all participants maintain active student status throughout the competition period.
eligibility.consentCondition: For participants under the age of 18, participation is subject to the consent of a parent or legal guardian. By submitting an entry, the participant represents that such consent has been obtained.
eligibility.teamCondition: If any one (1) team member falls under Category B, the entire team will be registered under Category B.

finalists.title: Conditions for Top 60 Finalists
finalists.item1: The Organisers will make every reasonable effort to notify the Top 60 finalists via email to attend the final round and prize-giving ceremony, which will be held on 13 June 2026, Saturday at Sunway University, Bandar Sunway.
finalists.item2: The Organisers reserve the right to request written proof and age of any Competition winner before the prize is dispatched or collected.
finalists.covenant: By submitting the entry, the participants covenant to the Organisers that:
covenants: The participant is the author of the submitted work
The submitted work is original and unpublished
He/She has read and agreed to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Competition
All the information provided are true and accurate
To bear any consequences (including automatically be disqualified) in the event of breach of any covenants
finalists.useOfMaterials: All submissions must be original works created by the participating teams. By participating, teams grant Sunway College, Sunway University and Rakan Tutor the right to use participants' names, photos, and submitted materials for promotional, educational, and marketing purposes. Attendance at the prize-giving ceremony at Sunway University constitutes consent to be photographed or filmed for official promotional use.
finalists.pdpa: In compliance with the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) 2010, visit the official Sunway Education Group for more information.
finalists.mediaConsent: The winner agrees to allow Sunway and its advertising and promotional agencies to publish his/her name and/or photographs and/or winner's participating photographs and/or essay or any parts thereof without further compensation and notice.
finalists.judgingFinal: Judges' decision is final and not subject to appeal. No correspondence will be entertained.
finalists.amendments: The Organisers reserve the right to amend, terminate or add on to the terms and conditions at any time by placing a notice at the relevant website or any designated area.
finalists.representative: In special situations, and subject to the Organisers' absolute discretion, the Competition winner may nominate a representative to collect the prize on his/her behalf. The representative will be required to present written authorisation from the winner and identification which includes a photograph for both the winner and the representative.

originality.title: Originality & Rights
originality.desc: All submissions must be original works created by the participating teams. The use of generative AI is permitted as a tool for brainstorming, research, and technical assistance, but the core solution and presentation must represent the team's own creative and intellectual effort.
originality.item1: Submissions found to be plagiarised or using unauthorised intellectual property will be disqualified immediately.
originality.item2: By participating, teams grant the Organisers a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual licence to use, reproduce, and display the submitted materials for promotional and educational purposes.

liability.title: Liability & Code of Conduct
liability.limitationDesc: To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Organisers shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, or consequential loss, damage, or injury arising from participation in the Competition or attendance at event venues. The Organisers are not responsible for technical failures, lost submissions, or service interruptions.
liability.ethicsDesc: Participants must behave professionally. The Organisers reserve the right to disqualify any team for plagiarism, unethical use of AI (e.g., generating deepfakes or offensive content), harassment, or any form of cheating.
liability.indemnity: Indemnity: Participants agree to indemnify and hold harmless Sunway University, Sunway College, and Rakan Tutor against any and all claims, costs, or liabilities arising from the participant's breach of these terms or any infringement of third-party intellectual property rights.
liability.notResponsible: The Organisers will not be responsible or liable for:
notResponsibleItems: Any delay and/or failure in participating in this Competition due to any reason beyond the control of the Organisers; or
Any injury, loss, problem, damages or costs of any kind suffered resulting from participation in this Competition or in result of accepting or utilising the prizes awarded.

bursary.title: Conditions for Bursary
bursary.desc: This bursary is to be used as tuition fee waiver for Sunway University/ Sunway College (KL) into following programmes:
bursary.catADesc: The bursary is only applicable to Pre-University or Diploma programmes (except Diploma in Nursing) offered at Sunway College/ Sunway University.
bursary.catBDesc: The bursary is only applicable to Undergraduate Degree programmes at Sunway University (except the Doctor of Medicine and Bachelor of Nursing).
bursary.item1: This bursary awarded is in the form of tuition fee deduction, it is not a cash award. In the event where the bursary amount awarded is more than the tuition fee payable of the year, the recipient will be awarded up to a maximum tuition fee payable for the year only. The balance of bursary quantum is not transferable to/ for any other type of fee payment nor refundable in cash.
bursary.item2: The bursary ONLY covers tuition fee category. General fees, deposits, external examination fees, tuition fees incurred in the event of repeat or re-sit subjects and other additional fees will be borne by the bursary winner.
bursary.item3: In the event that another bursary or scholarship is awarded to the recipient at Sunway College (KL)/ Sunway University, the recipient will be entitled to ONLY one bursary or scholarship at a time.
bursary.item4: For students who are eligible for several forms of sibling discount or staff discount by Sunway Education Group or Sunway Group, the bursary will be applicable on the net balance of the tuition fee payable after the deduction of the relevant discount.
bursary.item5: This bursary is subject to meeting minimum entry requirements of applied programme.
bursary.item6: The disbursement of bursary will be distributed in equal amount between the first two semesters of the programme. Balance of the scholarship will be deducted from the 3rd semester's tuition fees payable for the year. (Applicable to the Degree programmes with 3 semesters in academic year).
bursary.item7: In the event that the recipient withdraws from the programme, is suspended or terminated by Sunway University or Sunway College (KL) at any point in time, before the completion of the programme, the bursary shall be automatically terminated.
bursary.item8: The bursary recipient will need to attach the original Bursary Certificate along with the Application Form to the Admissions office at Sunway College (KL)/ Sunway University to confirm the acceptance of this bursary offered during registration.
bursary.item9: The validity date for this bursary is stated on the Bursary Certificate. This bursary will be void and terminated automatically when the validity date is due.
bursary.item10: Sunway College (KL)/ Sunway University reserves the right to alter, add or delete any of the terms and conditions and the bursary scheme at any point in time as and when Sunway College (KL)/ Sunway University deems necessary.
bursary.item11: In the event of a dispute on the eligibility for the bursary, the decision made by the Sunway Bursary Committee is final.
bursary.item12: Sunway College (KL)/ Sunway University reserves the right to involve the bursary recipients in interviews, advertisements, photography or other publicity related activities for the purpose of promoting the education institution.

contact.title: Contact For More Information
contact.desc: If you have any questions regarding the bursary or the competition terms and conditions, please reach out to:

hero.title: Privacy Policy
hero.desc: The National AI Competition (NAIC) 2026 is committed to protecting your privacy and handling your data transparently.

tldr.title: TL;DR
tldr.desc: We collect necessary information to run NAIC 2026. This includes registration details, team info, and student status. We never sell your data and only share it with partners involved in organising or judging the competition. For participants under 18, we require parental/guardian consent.

minors.title: Minors' Privacy
minors.desc: Given that NAIC 2026 involves students as young as 13 (Category A), we take additional precautions to protect their privacy.
items: We do not knowingly collect personal information from individuals under 18 without verifiable parental or guardian consent.
We only collect the minimum amount of personal data necessary for competition administration and prize distribution.
Parents or guardians have the right to review the personal data collected from their child and request its deletion.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.p1: This Privacy Policy explains how Rakan Tutor and Sunway University (collectively, "the Organisers", "we", "us", or "our") handle personal data when you register for and participate in the National AI Competition (NAIC) 2026.
intro.p2: By registering for NAIC 2026, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy. We adhere to the {{pdpaLink}} of Malaysia.

collect.title: What We Collect
collect.desc: We collect information that you provide directly to us during registration, including:

items.label: Account & Contact Info
items.desc: Full name, email address, and phone number.

items.label: Identification
items.desc: IC or Passport Number (for verification and certificate issuance).

items.label: Education Details
items.desc: School or institution name, current qualification, and expected graduation date.

items.label: Team Information
items.desc: Team name, selected track, and category.

items.label: Advisor Information
items.desc: Name, relationship, and contact details of your team's advisor.

use.title: How We Use Your Data
use.desc: We use your personal data for the following purposes:
items: To process and manage your competition registration
To communicate important updates and event details
To verify participant eligibility and category placement
To issue certificates of participation and achievement
To facilitate competition judging and mentorship
To improve the competition experience and reporting

media.title: Media & Photography
media.desc: Photography and videography will be conducted during the prize-giving ceremony at Sunway University.
items: Images and recordings of participants may be used for promotional, archival, and educational purposes by the Organisers.
Visual media is stored securely and is only accessible by authorised marketing and administrative personnel.
If you wish to opt-out of having your photograph published, please notify the on-site event staff during registration at the venue.

disclosure.title: Data Disclosure
disclosure.desc: We do not sell your personal data. We disclose data only to:
items: Event Organisers: Authorised staff from Rakan Tutor and Sunway University.
Judges & Mentors: Professional partners involved in the evaluation process.
Service Providers: Hosting and data management partners (e.g., Google Cloud/Sheets).

security.title: Data Security
security.desc: We use industry-standard security measures to protect your data. This includes encryption in transit and at rest for our primary databases.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.desc: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact the organisers at:
